results.title: How to Trigger User Interviews from PostHog Events - UserCall
results.content:
## Pre-filtering with where

Use where when the event name alone is too broad. It filters on the source side before events reach Usercall — excluded events don't appear in history or suggestions.

Trait-based rules require identify() to run first.

```
window.__usercallSetup.bindPostHog = { allowlist: ["newly_signed_up"], where: { properties: { entrypoint: "pricing_page" }, traits: { plan: "trial" } } };
```

This triggers interviews only for trial users who signed up via the pricing page, not every signup.

## Custom events

Use this instead of the PostHog binding if you want to trigger from your own app code.

```
window.usercall && window.usercall.trigger("pricing_feedback_requested", { reason: "too_expensive", page: "pricing" });
```

## Webhook delivery [...] PostHog shows what users do, not why. Research triggers close that gap — when a PostHog event fires, Usercall invites the user to a 2-5 min AI-moderated interview. Responses are synthesized into themes, not raw transcripts.

## Why Generic Event Triggers Fail

The same event-based logic that triggers interviews can power broader qualitative feedback collection. See our guide to event-triggered user feedback for the full pattern.

Most teams trigger interviews on too many events, too late, or with no user context. That creates noise, burns invitation volume, and gives you vague answers disconnected from the product moment that caused the behavior. [...] sacrificing rigor. The platform combines AI-moderated voice interviews with structured, researcher-controlled thematic analysis workflows. His work focuses on bridging traditional qualitative methodology with modern AI systems—ensuring speed and scale do not compromise nuance or research integrity. LinkedIn:

results.title: UserTesting vs Usercall AI - Best user research and usability testing platform Tool Comparison
results.content:
# UserTesting Alternative: Comparison vs Usercall AI

Looking for an alternative to UserTesting? Choosing the right user research and usability testing platform requires comparing features, pricing, ease of use, and suitability for your specific research needs. Below is a comprehensive comparison between UserTesting and Usercall AI to help you make an informed decision.

Try Usercall Free

## UserTesting & Usercall AI Alternative Overview

Usercall is an AI-powered qualitative research platform that helps teams collect and analyze user feedback at scale. It combines interviews, surveys, and behavioral context with automated analysis to turn unstructured data into clear themes, insights, and evidence-backed decisions. [...] Usercall includes an AI chat feature that lets you ask questions directly against your uploaded qualitative data and get instant answers grounded in the source material. UserTesting does not offer this capability, meaning teams using UserTesting still need to manually review recordings and notes to find specific insights rather than querying them conversationally.

Looking for more options? See our full guide to UserTesting alternatives — comparing UserTesting against 5+ tools by price, features, and use case.

results.title: Research Triggers: Capture Feedback at Product Moments - UserCall
results.content:
# Understand why users drop off—in hours, not weeks

Product analytics shows what users do.   
‍Usercall shows why, using short AI-moderated conversations triggered at key moments, grounded in what users just did.

> 3-5 conversations → actionable drop-off insights in 24 hours

Start capturing insights →See 2-min demo interview

## What Are Research Triggers?

.svg)

Product   
Event

.svg)

User   
Intercept

Micro   
Interview

Themes   
& Insights

When a product event occurs, Usercall can automatically invite targeted users to a short micro voice or text based interview while the experience is still fresh. This turns product moments into continuous user discovery. Each response comes with context, so teams can connect what users say with who they are and what they just did. [...] #### When should I use research triggers instead of surveys?

Research triggers work best when you want to understand context, motivations, or confusion. Short surveys are useful for ratings or quick feedback, while voice or text interviews with real time probing and follow-ups reveal deeper insights about what users were trying to do.

#### Will this interrupt the user experience?

Invitations are lightweight and can be configured with frequency limits, user targeting, throttling rules. This ensures users aren’t repeatedly prompted and you can precisely target the right people at the right time.

#### Can research triggers work for B2C, SaaS or e-commerce products? [...] Features Image

## Common Research Triggers

Research triggers help teams understand key moments across the user journey. Research triggers work across SaaS, fintech, consumer marketplaces, e-commerce and more.

### Turn Support Moments Into Insights

Talk to customers when they contact support. Understand the deeper product issues behind support requests.See what users needed at support moments →

### Capture Feedback When Issues Happen

Talk to users when they hit an issue, error, or failed action. Understand what they were trying to do and where the experience breaks.See why issues happen →

### Understand Checkout or Purchase Abandonment

results.title: Best Trigger.dev Alternatives (2026): Open-Source
results.content:
Go with Trigger.dev

Apache 2.0 license. Self-host with Docker for free with no feature limitations.

You need advanced durable step functions

Go with Inngest

Inngest's step functions are more advanced. They sleep between steps and handle complex orchestration.

You just need simple async message delivery

Go with QStash

HTTP POST to deliver a message. No SDK, no complexity. Perfect for basic async tasks.

You want a battle-tested Redis-based queue

Go with BullMQ

Mature, self-hosted, and handles millions of jobs. You need to manage Redis but get full control.

You're in the AWS ecosystem and want managed durable workflows

Go with AWS Step Functions

Visual state machine builder with native Lambda, SQS, DynamoDB, and 200+ AWS service integrations. No infrastructure to manage. [...] Trigger.dev v3 introduced a major architectural change: jobs now run on dedicated long-running compute instead of your serverless functions. This means your jobs are no longer limited by serverless execution timeouts (like Vercel's 10-second limit on the Hobby plan). Jobs can run for minutes or hours, making Trigger.dev suitable for video processing, large data imports, and other long-running tasks. The v3 runtime includes checkpointing, which saves job state so it can resume after interruptions. [...] BuildMVPFast Logo

# Best Trigger.dev Alternatives in 2026

Compare Trigger.dev alternatives: open-source background jobs, pricing, and features for 2026

## What is Trigger.dev?

Trigger.dev is an open-source background job platform for TypeScript. Write jobs that run reliably with automatic retries, scheduling, and integrations. Self-host for free or use the managed cloud. The free tier on the managed cloud includes 5,000 runs per month. The open-source license (Apache 2.0) means you can self-host with unlimited runs and no feature limitations.
